title: Wikipedia was in read-only mode following mass admin account compromise
summary:
The Wikimedia Status page provides real-time operational status and historical incident reports for Wikipedia and other wikis. Currently, all systems, including reading and editing functionalities, are operational. Users can subscribe to updates via email, Slack, webhooks, or RSS/Atom feeds.

The most significant recent incident occurred on March 5-6, 2026, when Wikis were in read-only mode. This issue began around 15:36 UTC on March 5, was identified, and a fix was implemented, returning wikis to read-write mode by 17:36 UTC. Initially, some functionalities remained disabled, but most user scripting capabilities were restored later on March 6, with the incident fully resolved by that day.

Other recent service disruptions included:
*   March 3, 2026: Delays in edits to Wikipedia due to a database issue, which was quickly resolved.
*   February 26 & 25, 2026: Incidents involving degraded performance and issues accessing wikis.
*   February 20, 2026: Issues and slowdowns specifically affecting users in Europe.

Between these incidents, several days were reported with no issues. The page also aims to display system metrics such as request volume, connectivity errors, and response times.
chinese_title: 维基百科在大规模管理员账户失陷后进入了只读模式。
chinese_summary:
维基媒体状态页提供维基百科及其他维基项目的实时运行状态和历史故障报告。目前，包括阅读和编辑功能在内的所有系统均正常运行。用户可以通过电子邮件、Slack、webhook 或 RSS/Atom 源订阅更新。

最近最重大的事件发生在2026年3月5日至6日，当时维基项目处于只读模式。此问题始于3月5日世界协调时15:36左右，经识别并实施修复后，维基项目于世界协调时17:36前恢复读写模式。最初，部分功能仍处于禁用状态，但大部分用户脚本功能于3月6日晚些时候恢复，此次事件也在当天得到全面解决。

其他最近的服务中断包括：
*   2026年3月3日：由于数据库问题导致维基百科编辑延迟，问题很快得到解决。
*   2026年2月26日和25日：涉及性能下降和维基项目访问问题的事件。
*   2026年2月20日：专门影响欧洲用户的故障和速度变慢问题。

在这些事件之间，曾有数日报告无问题。该页面还旨在显示系统指标，例如请求量、连接错误和响应时间。

title: Google Workspace CLI
summary:
The Google Workspace CLI (`gws`) provides a unified command-line interface for all Google Workspace APIs, including Drive, Gmail, Calendar, Sheets, and Chat. Designed for both human users and AI agents, it boasts a dynamic command surface that reads Google's Discovery Service at runtime, automatically updating with new API endpoints.

Key features include structured JSON output for all responses, zero boilerplate, detailed `--help` for every resource, `--dry-run` to preview requests, and auto-pagination. It bundles over 100 AI agent skills, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to manage Workspace effectively without custom tooling, and integrates with platforms like OpenClaw and the Gemini CLI Extension.

Installation is typically via `npm install -g @googleworkspace/cli` (requiring Node.js 18+ and a Google Cloud project). Authentication is flexible, supporting `gws auth setup` (leveraging `gcloud`), manual OAuth setup, existing access tokens, credentials files, or service accounts, suitable for local development, CI, and server environments. Important considerations include adding test users for unverified OAuth apps to prevent "Access blocked" errors.

Advanced capabilities include multipart uploads, configurable pagination, and integration with Google Cloud Model Armor for response sanitization. The project is under active development and is not an officially supported Google product, with troubleshooting guidance provided for common authentication and API enablement issues.
chinese_title: Google Workspace 命令行界面
chinese_summary:
Google Workspace CLI（`gws`）为所有 Google Workspace API（包括云端硬盘、Gmail、日历、表格和聊天）提供统一的命令行界面。它专为人类用户和 AI 代理设计，拥有动态命令界面，可在运行时读取 Google 的 Discovery 服务，自动更新新的 API 端点。

主要功能包括所有响应的结构化 JSON 输出、零样板代码、每个资源的详细 `--help` 命令、用于预览请求的 `--dry-run` 以及自动分页。它捆绑了 100 多个 AI 代理技能，使大型语言模型（LLM）无需自定义工具即可有效管理 Workspace，并与 OpenClaw 和 Gemini CLI 扩展等平台集成。

安装通常通过 `npm install -g @googleworkspace/cli` 进行（需要 Node.js 18+ 和一个 Google Cloud 项目）。身份验证灵活，支持 `gws auth setup`（利用 `gcloud`）、手动 OAuth 设置、现有访问令牌、凭据文件或服务帐号，适用于本地开发、CI 和服务器环境。重要注意事项包括为未经验证的 OAuth 应用添加测试用户，以防止出现“访问被阻止”错误。

高级功能包括分段上传、可配置分页以及与 Google Cloud Model Armor 集成以进行响应净化。该项目正在积极开发中，并非 Google 官方支持的产品，并提供针对常见身份验证和 API 启用问题的故障排除指南。

title: 10% of Firefox crashes are caused by bitflips
summary: Gabriele Svelto, a key figure in the Firefox development community, has revealed that approximately 10% of Firefox browser crashes are attributable to bitflips. This significant insight was made possible by a detection method Svelto designed "a few years ago" specifically to identify these subtle memory errors. His work highlights bitflips as a notable, measurable factor contributing to browser instability and software reliability issues.
chinese_title: 10% 的 Firefox 崩溃是由比特翻转引起的
chinese_summary: Firefox开发社区的核心人物加布里埃莱·斯维尔托透露，大约10%的Firefox浏览器崩溃可归因于位翻转。这一重要发现得益于斯维尔托“几年前”设计的一种检测方法，该方法专门用于识别这些细微的内存错误。他的工作突出显示，位翻转是导致浏览器不稳定和软件可靠性问题的一个显著且可衡量的因素。

title: Global warming has accelerated significantly
summary:
A new preprint study by Stefan Rahmstorf and Grant Foster, titled "Global Warming has Accelerated Significantly," argues that the rate of global warming has markedly increased, particularly since 2015.

Previous analyses found it difficult to confirm an acceleration in global warming at a 95% confidence level due to the masking effects of natural temperature variability. This research addresses that limitation by adjusting global temperature data for the influence of three key natural variability factors: El Niño, volcanism, and solar variation.

After accounting for these natural influences, the study's adjusted data reveals a significant finding: global temperature rose significantly faster after 2015 than in any previous 10-year period since 1945. This suggests that once natural fluctuations are filtered out, the underlying acceleration of global warming becomes clearly evident.

It is important to note that this work is a preprint and has not yet undergone peer review by a journal.
chinese_title: 全球变暖显著加速。
chinese_summary:
Stefan Rahmstorf和Grant Foster发布的一项题为《全球变暖显著加速》的新预印本研究认为，全球变暖的速度已显著加快，尤其是在2015年之后。

此前的分析发现，由于自然温度变异性的掩盖效应，难以在95%的置信水平上确认全球变暖的加速。这项研究通过调整全球温度数据，排除了厄尔尼诺现象、火山活动和太阳活动变化这三个关键自然变异因素的影响，从而解决了这一局限。

在排除了这些自然影响后，该研究调整后的数据显示了一项重要发现：2015年之后，全球气温的上升速度明显快于1945年以来任何一个此前的十年期。这表明，一旦自然波动被滤除，全球变暖的潜在加速就会变得清晰可见。

需要指出的是，这项工作是一份预印本，尚未经过期刊的同行评审。

title: System76 on Age Verification Laws
summary:
System76 CEO Carl Richell critiques new age verification laws in Colorado, California, and New York, arguing they are ineffective, detrimental to liberty, and stifle innovation. He highlights children's innate resourcefulness in circumventing digital restrictions, as demonstrated by his own child bypassing AI limitations.

Colorado and California bills require operating systems to report age brackets, implicitly discouraging under-18s from creating independent computer accounts. Richell argues these laws are flawed because they rely on easily falsifiable self-reporting and can be bypassed by tech-savvy youth. He stresses that limiting early access to computing restricts future tech leaders, many of whom began experimenting as children.

New York's proposed bill is more extreme, mandating adults prove their age for all internet-enabled devices, potentially eliminating privacy through third-party verification. Richell notes its problematic wording could even make Linux distribution downloaders "device manufacturers," creating absurd liability in open-source ecosystems.

System76 champions decentralized platforms like Linux as crucial for personal liberty and fostering human potential, contrasting them with centralized platforms that control users. Richell concludes that the solution lies not in legal restrictions but in educating children to navigate the digital world responsibly, rather than denying access or encouraging them to lie. While System76 will comply, they view these age verification mandates as "folly" and hope for their repeal or unconstitutionality.
chinese_title: System76 谈年龄验证法
chinese_summary:
System76首席执行官Carl Richell批评科罗拉多州、加利福尼亚州和纽约州的新年龄验证法案，认为这些法案无效、损害自由并扼杀创新。他强调儿童天生具有规避数字限制的足智多谋，并以他自己的孩子绕过AI限制为例。

科罗拉多州和加利福尼亚州的法案要求操作系统报告年龄段，这间接阻碍了18岁以下用户创建独立的电脑账户。Richell认为这些法律存在缺陷，因为它们依赖于容易伪造的自我报告，并且可以被精通技术的年轻人绕过。他强调，限制儿童早期接触计算机技术会束缚未来的科技领袖，因为他们中的许多人都是从儿童时期开始进行实验的。

纽约州拟议的法案更为极端，要求成年人证明其所有互联网设备的用户年龄，这可能通过第三方验证消除隐私。Richell指出，其有问题的措辞甚至可能将Linux发行版下载者定义为“设备制造商”，从而在开源生态系统中造成荒谬的责任。

System76倡导像Linux这样的去中心化平台，认为它们对个人自由和激发人类潜力至关重要，并将其与控制用户的中心化平台进行对比。Richell总结说，解决方案不在于法律限制，而在于教育孩子负责任地驾驭数字世界，而不是拒绝他们访问或鼓励他们撒谎。尽管System76将遵守这些规定，但他们认为这些年龄验证要求是“愚蠢的”，并希望它们被废除或被判定违宪。

title: The L in "LLM" Stands for Lying
summary:
The author critically examines the hype around LLMs, arguing that despite significant investment, they haven't revolutionized software development, instead often yielding mediocre results. The core contention is that LLMs produce "forgeries" – imitations lacking authenticity, similar to fake art or documents. When these forgeries are used as substitutes for genuine work, they degrade craft, devalue expertise, and threaten intellectual property.

This "vibe-coding" trend negatively impacts open-source projects with "slop-coded" contributions and introduces "run-of-the-mill mediocrity" in professional settings, masking a lack of genuine understanding or unique perspective. The author contrasts this with the video game industry, where consumers successfully pushed back against unlabeled AI content, valuing artistic integrity and unique vision. Conversely, the open-source ethos in code, while promoting re-use, can facilitate plagiarism of design and ideas when combined with generative AI.

The article dismisses AI content labeling as insufficient, arguing that true source attribution is a technical impossibility for current LLMs. The proposed solution is to require LLMs to perform correct source attribution with inference; without it, AI output should be treated as a forgery, exposing uncredited copy-pasted code and upholding intellectual property. The "L" in LLM, the author suggests, stands for "Lying" due to its deceptive lack of authenticity and origin.
chinese_title: LLM中的L代表撒谎
chinese_summary:
The author critically examines the hype around LLMs, arguing that despite significant investment, they haven't revolutionized software development, instead often yielding mediocre results. The core contention is that LLMs produce "forgeries" – imitations lacking authenticity, similar to fake art or documents. When these forgeries are used as substitutes for genuine work, they degrade craft, devalue expertise, and threaten intellectual property.

This "vibe-coding" trend negatively impacts open-source projects with "slop-coded" contributions and introduces "run-of-the-mill mediocrity" in professional settings, masking a lack of genuine understanding or unique perspective. The author contrasts this with the video game industry, where consumers successfully pushed back against unlabeled AI content, valuing artistic integrity and unique vision. Conversely, the open-source ethos in code, while promoting re-use, can facilitate plagiarism of design and ideas when combined with generative AI.

The article dismisses AI content labeling as insufficient, arguing that true source attribution is a technical impossibility for current LLMs. The proposed solution is to require LLMs to perform correct source attribution with inference; without it, AI output should be treated as a forgery, exposing uncredited copy-pasted code and upholding intellectual property. The "L" in LLM, the author suggests, stands for "Lying" due to its deceptive lack of authenticity and origin.
